2605.20815.title: GraphRAG on Consumer Hardware: Benchmarking Local LLMs for Healthcare EHR Schema Retrieval
2605.20815.paper_summary: Graph-based Retrieval Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) extends retrieval-augmented generation to support structured reasoning over complex corpora, but its reliability under resource-constrained, privacy-sensitive deployments remains unclear. In healthcare, where Electronic Health Record (EHR) data is complex and strictly regulated, reliance on cloud-based large language models (LLMs) introduces challenges in cost, latency, and compliance. In this work, we present a systematic evaluation of GraphRAG for EHR schema retrieval using locally deployed open-source LLMs. We implement the Microsoft GraphRAG pipeline on real-world EHR schema documentation and benchmark four models, including Llama 3.1 (8B), Mistral (7B), Qwen 2.5 (7B), and Phi-4-mini (3.8B), each deployed via Ollama on a single consumer GPU (8 GB VRAM). We evaluate indexing efficiency, knowledge graph construction, query latency, answer quality, and hallucination under both global and local retrieval modes. Our results reveal substantial differences: Llama 3.1 produces the richest knowledge graph (1,172 entities), Qwen 2.5 achieves the best answer quality (3.3/5), Phi-4-mini fails to complete the pipeline due to structured-output errors, and Mistral exhibits degenerate repetition behavior. We further show that GraphRAG exhibits a practical capacity threshold, where models below approximately 7B parameters fail to reliably produce valid structured outputs and cannot complete the pipeline. In addition, indexing and answer quality are decoupled across models, and local retrieval consistently outperforms global summarization in both latency and factual grounding, with reduced hallucination. These findings demonstrate that GraphRAG is feasible on consumer hardware while highlighting the importance of model selection and retrieval design for robust deployment in regulated settings.
2605.20815.paper_summary_zh: Graph-based Retrieval Augmented Generation (GraphRAG) 擴展了檢索增強生成，以支持對複雜語料庫的結構化推理，但其在資源受限和隱私敏感的部署下的可靠性仍不明確。<lb/>在醫療保健領域，電子健康記錄 (EHR) 數據複雜且受到嚴格監管，依賴基於雲的大型語言模型 (LLMs) 在成本、延遲和合規性方面帶來挑戰。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們針對 EHR 架構檢索，使用本地部署的開源 LLMs 對 GraphRAG 進行系統評估。<lb/>我們在真實世界的 EHR 架構文檔上實施了 Microsoft GraphRAG 管道，並基準測試了四個模型，包括 Llama 3.1 (8B)、Mistral (7B)、Qwen 2.5 (7B) 和 Phi-4-mini (3.8B)，每個模型都通過 Ollama 在單個消費者 GPU (8 GB VRAM) 上部署。<lb/>我們評估了索引效率、知識圖譜構建、查詢延遲、答案質量和幻覺，並在全球和本地檢索模式下進行比較。<lb/>我們的結果顯示出顯著差異：Llama 3.1 產生了最豐富的知識圖譜 (1,172 個實體)，Qwen 2.5 實現了最佳答案質量 (3.3/5)，Phi-4-mini 由於結構化輸出錯誤未能完成管道，而 Mistral 則表現出退化重複行為。<lb/>我們進一步顯示 GraphRAG 具有實際的容量閾值，約 7B 參數以下的模型無法可靠地產生有效的結構化輸出，並且無法完成管道。<lb/>此外，索引和答案質量在模型之間是解耦的，本地檢索在延遲和事實基礎上始終優於全球摘要，並減少了幻覺。<lb/>這些發現表明 GraphRAG 在消費者硬體上是可行的，同時突顯了模型選擇和檢索設計在受監管環境中穩健部署的重要性。
2605.20815.author: Peter Fernandes et.al.
